title: opt_clean doesn't remove init bits when it removes FFs

features.name: partially used multi-bit register with initial value
features.description: Code should include a multi-bit register assigned in an initial block where only a subset of bits is connected to an output, leaving other bits as dead FFs that an optimizer may remove while leaving dangling init attributes

features.name: multi-bit register initial block with clocked always
features.description: Code should include a multi-bit register initialized in an initial block and updated in a clocked always block using non-blocking assignment, representing a synthesizable FF pattern with reset state

features.name: single-bit output selecting from multi-bit register
features.description: Code should include a continuous assignment that selects a single bit from a multi-bit register, leaving other register bits with no downstream fanout and thus candidates for dead logic removal
